design_philosophy.name: Tech-Utility Precision
design_philosophy.description: A professional, high-contrast aesthetic that blends automotive reliability with modern SaaS clarity. Uses deep purple for brand authority and slate grays for technical data.
principles: Data Density with Breathing Room: High information density for lists, but generous padding inside containers.
Tactile Controls: Buttons and inputs should feel 'pressable' and substantial, suitable for usage in a garage environment.
Visual Hierarchy: Color is used strictly for status and primary actions. Text hierarchy defines the rest.

instructions: Replace the simple logo circle with a modern header layout.
Use a 'Split' visual: Top 35% is a subtle purple gradient curve or pattern, Bottom 65% is the white content area.
Logo should be prominent in the top area with white text.
Input fields should use the 'Input' component style defined above.
The 'Continue' button should be a full-width gradient button (Purple to Deep Purple).
OTP inputs should be individual boxes with rounded corners (12px), slightly larger (60x60), with a focus ring color of #6B21A8.
Header: Remove the simple row. Create a 'Dashboard Header' with a greeting in large bold type (24px) and the date/location in smaller text.
Tabs: Replace the pill tabs with a 'Segmented Control' look or a cleaner 'Underline' tab system with a purple active indicator.
- Header: Vehicle Number (Bold, 18px) on the left. Status Badge (Pill shape, smaller text) on the right.
- Body: 2-column layout. Left: Car Icon + Model Name. Right: Date & Time.
- Footer: 'Accept' and 'Reject' buttons should be side-by-side. 'Accept' gets the primary purple gradient. 'Reject' gets a subtle ghost style with red text.
- Add a 'Location' row with a map pin icon that is clickable.
Empty State: Use a more illustrative icon or a soft purple background circle for the icon.
Input Section: Make the vehicle number input look like a 'License Plate' (Yellow or White background, border, specific font style if possible, or just bold uppercase).
Verify Button: Large, floating action button or fixed bottom bar style.
Vehicle Details Card: When verified, show a 'Digital Manifest' card. Dark header with 'Verified' status, white body with grid details.
Typography: Use Monospace font for the Vehicle Number display to prevent ambiguity (0 vs O).

universal_guidelines: Ensure all touch targets are at least 44x44 points.
Use 'LinearGradient' for primary buttons to give depth.
Avoid pure black (#000000). Use Slate 900 (#0F172A) for text.
All interactive elements must have 'activeOpacity={0.8}' for feedback.
